title: Pomaderris flabellaris
intro: Pomaderris flabellaris, also known as the fan-leaved pomaderris, is a shrub native to eastern Australia and New South Wales. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Pomaderris flabellaris is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pomaderris flabellaris is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Pomaderris flabellaris is classified in the family Rhamnaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the buckthorn or alder family. This family consists of around 50-60 genera and approximately 900 species. The genus Pomaderris is endemic to Australia and New Zealand, and it contains around 20 species. The classification of Pomaderris flabellaris is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the order Rosales. Pomaderris flabellaris is a species of flowering plant that is native to New South Wales in Australia.
